title: Cencora, Inc. (COR) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Cencora (COR) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Projected at 8.0%, reflecting Cencora's steady cash generation capabilities, driven by the increasing volume of specialty drugs, consistent share repurchases, and operational efficiencies.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 7.5% discount rate is appropriate given the defensive, non-cyclical nature of Cencora's pharmaceutical distribution business, low beta (0.69), and relatively predictable cash flows.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.5% terminal rate is conservative, generally tracking long-term inflation and the continuous underlying growth in healthcare utilization, limited by the sheer existing scale of the business.

thesisParagraphs: Cencora's primary advantage lies in its sheer, unavoidable scale. Operating as a crucial intermediary in the pharmaceutical supply chain, it benefits from an oligopoly dynamic alongside McKesson and Cardinal Health. This scale creates a massive barrier to entry, ensuring predictable, non-cyclical cash flow regardless of broader economic conditions. While distribution margins are famously thin, the sheer volume of drugs moving through its network translates to substantial absolute free cash flow.

Furthermore, the company's valuation is increasingly supported by the ongoing shift towards specialty pharmaceuticals. These complex, high-value therapies command higher margins and require specialized handling and logistics. Cencora's strategic focus on expanding its capabilities in this area, combined with steady international growth, provides a clear pathway for sustained mid-single-digit free cash flow growth, supporting a modest undervaluation at current prices.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini project an 8.0% FCF growth rate for Cencora?
faq.answer: This rate is based on the continued structural tailwinds in healthcare, specifically the increasing volume and approval of complex specialty medications that require Cencora's specialized distribution networks, paired with consistent margin improvements.

faq.question: Why is the discount rate relatively low at 7.5%?
faq.answer: Cencora operates in a highly defensive sector. Demand for pharmaceuticals is inelastic, providing the company with incredibly stable and predictable cash flows, thereby reducing its risk profile and justifying a lower discount rate.

faq.question: What is the biggest risk to this valuation?
faq.answer: Significant legislative changes targeting drug pricing in the US, or the loss of a major contract with a large pharmacy chain, could negatively impact distribution volumes and margins.
